task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the project directory and inspects the configuration files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the agent changing directory to ~/projects/db-backup/ and using commands like 'ls', 'cat', or a text editor to view the Dockerfile, entrypoint script, or crontab.

task_completion.description: The agent modifies the configuration to ensure the API_KEY environment variable is inherited by the cron job.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing modifications to the project files (e.g., entrypoint.sh, Dockerfile, or crontab) that export or pass the API_KEY environment variable to the cron environment.

task_completion.description: The agent modifies the cron job to redirect its stdout and stderr to the container logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the cron job command being updated to append redirection to the container's standard output/error (e.g., adding '> /proc/1/fd/1 2>&1' or similar).

task_completion.description: The agent rebuilds or restarts the container to ensure the solution is persistent across service restarts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands like 'docker-compose up -d --build', 'docker-compose restart', or 'docker build' being executed in the project directory.

task_completion.description: The agent verifies the fix by checking the container logs for the success message.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of 'docker logs db-backup' (or similar log command) with the output clearly displaying the message 'Backup payload delivered successfully'.

integrity.description: The agent modifies the actual project files to make the changes persistent, rather than just running temporary commands inside the running container.
integrity.visual_evidence: File edits should be visible in the host's ~/projects/db-backup/ directory (e.g., using nano/vim on the host) rather than only using 'docker exec'.

integrity.description: The agent does not manually print the success message to fake completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The success message 'Backup payload delivered successfully' must come from the actual 'docker logs' output, not from an 'echo' command typed by the agent.

integrity.description: The agent checks the actual Docker container logs to verify the fix.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must run 'docker logs db-backup' (or equivalent docker-compose logs command) rather than manually executing the backup script in the terminal to see the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is '/home/ga/projects/db-backup'. The container is named 'db-backup'. The required environment variable to be inherited by cron is 'API_KEY'. A successful fix will result in 'docker logs db-backup' displaying the message 'Backup payload delivered successfully'.
